sections.paragraph: ἅπαξ λεγόμ., in my judgment, kindred to the roots יָרַע, יָרֵא, pr. to tremble; hence to be frightened, like the Arabic وَرِهَ. Hence תִּֽרְהוּ (2 Codd. תִּירְהוּ), Isa 44:8, i.q. תָּשֹׁמּוּ. LXX. μὴ πλανᾶσθε. All the other versions express the signification of fearing, as though it were the same as תִּירְאוּ.
